items.summary: Tuhk re-surfaces in Fintech.ca's 'Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch in 2026' (still circulating in search). Described as building a collaborative payments platform enabling secure, real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat global fraud — a direct structural parallel to Revaly's RTN bi-directional data rails. No new funding or product announcements surfaced this week, but their continued media presence in fintech press signals active PR investment.
items.source: Fintech.ca — Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch 2026

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton called friendly fraud an 'epidemic' in mainstream Sky News coverage, and separately warned that agentic commerce will shift dispute patterns as AI systems move from recommending purchases to executing them — coverage spanning Finextra, Finopotamus, and Business Money. Chargebacks911 is dominating the agentic chargeback narrative across multiple outlets simultaneously. Visa confirmed it has completed secure AI-initiated transactions and is positioning 2026 as a key year for agent-driven commerce — legitimizing the category Chargebacks911 owns.
items.source: Sky News / Finextra / Finopotamus — Chargebacks911 Coverage

items.summary: New analysis from Toolient (published yesterday, Mar 16) argues that whether agentic commerce scales safely 'depends entirely on whether AI signals can stop agentic chargebacks before traditional fraud filters fail to detect them.' Agent-driven transactions are creating disputes that conventional card controls weren't architected for. This creates a new upstream signal layer — exactly where Revaly's RTN data rails play — and represents a net-new argument for real-time issuer-merchant data sharing.
items.source: Toolient.com — Fraud and Risk: AI Signals to Stop Agentic Chargebacks

items.summary: Vindicia's subscription recovery integration is being actively promoted by a new crypto payment gateway (via BraveNewCoin sponsored content, published ~Mar 12). The pitch: one API for card + crypto + digital wallets with Vindicia handling failed transaction recovery as a fallback layer. Vindicia is quietly expanding its recovery surface area into crypto and alternative payment rails — no announcement, but this is a tactical moat expansion that diversifies their recovery footprint beyond traditional card.

Mastercard's Recorded Future 2026 fraud report (newly published) declares that winning organizations will 'align leadership, fuse cyber and fraud intelligence, and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Cross-network signal sharing is now an explicit Mastercard-endorsed strategic priority, not an emerging concept. This is fourth consecutive week the theme surfaces in major press — increasing mainstream legitimacy for Revaly's RTN architecture and issuer connectivity story.
items.source: Mastercard.com / Recorded Future — Annual Payment Fraud Report 2026

items.summary: Kount (Equifax) featured in a new fraud detection software comparison guide published this week, citing $12K–$150K annual pricing range and 30+ fraud-focused patents. Kount's dual-model ML and chargeback management are positioned against the same subscription and eCommerce buyer segments as Revaly. With Equifax backing, Kount can absorb heavy R&D — but their identity/fraud angle still doesn't cover the proactive approval optimization layer Revaly's RTN rails unlock.
items.source: Supervizor.com — Financial Fraud Detection Software 2026 Comparison

items.summary: GlobeNewswire published the 'Subscription Billing Management Market Report 2026' (Mar 13) projecting a growing shift toward hybrid consumption models combining subscriptions and usage-based pricing through 2035. Vindicia named among key market participants alongside Chargebee, Recurly, Stripe, and Salesforce. The report signals the category is maturing from pure-subscription to complex billing logic — an architecture complexity gap Revaly's recovery layer sits on top of regardless of billing model.
items.source: GlobeNewswire — Subscription Billing Management Market Report 2026

items.summary: Outseta's 2026 SaaS billing platform roundup (updated 1 week ago) specifically highlights Recurly as using 'aggressive machine learning trained on data from companies like Twitch and Paramount+' to recover failed payments. Recurly's positioning focuses on preventing involuntary churn as a first-party feature within billing platforms — a bundled alternative to third-party recovery. This is the same buyer segment Revaly targets; incumbents like Recurly are increasingly framing recovery as table stakes, raising the bar for what standalone recovery vendors must prove.

The new Nacha ACH compliance mandate takes effect in 4 days (March 20, 2026), requiring merchants to ensure ACH workflows match card-level security and compliance standards. Combined with the Visa subscription mandate arriving April 18, subscription merchants face a two-punch compliance window in Q1/Q2 2026. This creates a natural outreach window for Revaly: merchants reviewing their payments stack for compliance will be receptive to conversations about recovery and approval optimization.
items.source: Payscout.com — 2026 Merchant Guide to Unified Payments

items.summary: Chargebacks911 earned additional mainstream press this week via Startups.co.uk (Feb 25 article surfacing in new search results) and TechRound — both citing Chargebacks911 as the authority on agentic AI chargeback risk. With Revaly's incoming board member Brad Wiskerchen coming from Chargebacks911, Revaly is uniquely positioned to bridge the gap between dispute prevention (Chargebacks911's lane) and payment approval optimization (Revaly's RTN lane) — a combined narrative no competitor currently owns.
items.source: Startups.co.uk / TechRound — Agentic AI Chargeback Coverage

items.summary: PYMNTS published a feature titled 'Merchants Start Chasing High Approvals Instead of Low Fees' — EPX/North exec quoted: 'For a larger merchant, a one or two percent improvement in approval rates can add millions of dollars in revenue a year.' Merchants are now treating payment optimization as a revenue driver, not a cost center. The article highlights 'active vaulting' and AI-guided transaction routing as the 2026 frontier. This is direct mainstream validation of Revaly's RTN/approval optimization thesis and talking points.

Chargebacks911 CTO Donald Kossmann published in Help Net Security: agentic AI creates 'intent drift' — agents act within permissions but produce outcomes users reject, creating a dispute gray zone existing controls weren't designed for. Chargebacks911 is now getting both CEO and CTO published in security and fintech press, cementing authority in agentic dispute category. Incoming Revaly board member Brad Wiskerchen comes from this company — Revaly could leverage this IP and positioning directly.

Vindicia featured prominently in multiple 2026 subscription billing platform roundups, with Vindicia Select described as 'an AI-driven service specifically designed to recover failed payment transactions.' No new product announcements, but continued strong category presence through SEO-optimized comparison content. Vindicia's market positioning remains focused on high-volume digital media — an overlapping segment with Revaly's publishing/media wins like AdvantageCS.

Finsi.ai published a 'Complete Guide to Failed Payment Recovery' targeting subscription businesses — covering decline codes, smart retry strategies, email sequences, and ROI calculation. Finsi.ai appears to be a newer AI-native entrant entering the recovery content/product space. Worth monitoring as an emerging competitor building inbound SEO presence in Revaly's core category.

Mastercard's Recorded Future annual fraud report (published week of Mar 10) states winning organizations will 'align leadership, fuse cyber and fraud intelligence, and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Cross-network signal sharing is now an explicit Mastercard-endorsed best practice — third consecutive week this theme surfaces in major press. Directly validates Revaly's RTN/issuer connectivity architecture as the emerging industry standard.

Tuhk named one of 10 Canadian fintech startups to watch in 2026 by Fintech.ca. Described as building a collaborative payments platform enabling real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat fraud — directly competing with Revaly's RTN bi-directional data rails thesis. Tuhk's approach mirrors Revaly's core moat: network-level trust signals shared upstream. Worth monitoring for overlap with Revaly's issuer partnerships and RTN narrative.
items.source: Fintech.ca — Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch 2026

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton quoted in mainstream fintech press (Finextra): 'The card wasn't stolen. The merchant didn't make a mistake. The agent did exactly what it was told.' Chargebacks911 is actively publishing content and briefing media on the agentic AI chargeback wave, reinforcing their positioning as the authority on emerging dispute categories. Brad Wiskerchen (incoming Revaly board member) brings this IP network directly to Revaly's board.

Kount (Equifax) continuing to be cited in 2026 fraud prevention roundups specifically for eCommerce and subscription-based businesses — framed as 'improving customer trust by preventing chargebacks and reducing payment fraud.' Kount's positioning increasingly overlaps with Revaly's approval optimization layer as Equifax-backed resources allow them to scale their identity graph. No new product announcements, but market citation frequency increasing.

Multiple independent industry sources in March 2026 cite 20-40% involuntary churn recovery rates as industry benchmark for AI-powered dunning flows. Subscription Commerce 2026 guide and G2's AI churn reduction expert survey both anchor this range, legitimizing Revaly's core recovery value proposition to a broader buyer audience. The narrative is mainstream — prospect education cost is decreasing for Revaly.

Customer Experience Dive published a feature on Butter Payments, citing their stat that involuntary churn costs subscription companies up to $440B annually. Butter claims 128 unique data points per transaction and 10% ARR improvement for clients. This is mainstream B2B press — not a trade pub — reaching CX and ops buyers who haven't traditionally been in the recovery category. Butter is actively expanding its buyer audience beyond payments teams.
